title: "Practical & logistical" (treatment travel, what to bring, talking to your team) — non-financial-advice
context: Ewing-Family-Guide is a free, plain-language guide for families, caregivers, and AYA patients facing a Ewing sarcoma diagnosis. It ONLY restates and organizes vetted-authority information (NCI/PDQ, COG, ESMO, CCLG), with a citation on every assertion, at a health-literacy reading level. This is HIGH risk-tier, patient-facing health content: nothing patient-facing ships until both a credentialed pediatric/sarcoma oncologist AND a patient/caregiver advocate sign off (version-scoped, either can block). No delivery partner, oncologist reviewer, or patient-advocate reviewer is yet secured. A backlog/future practical page (treatment travel, what to bring, talking to your team), drafted from vetted sources and explicitly giving no financial advice. Drafts are marked 'draft' and not published until dual sign-off. Binding cancer guardrails apply: open-access/aggregate/de-identified educational sources only; controlled-access (dbGaP, EGA, individual-level biobanks) and any identifiable patient data are out of scope; verify each source license; provenance on every assertion; no medical advice, diagnosis, or individual prognosis.
objective: Draft, strictly from vetted sources, the 'Practical & logistical' page (treatment travel, what to bring, talking to your team), giving no financial advice, pending dual sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Every assertion restates a vetted source and carries provenance (passes citation-coverage); <= grade 8 with standing 'not medical advice' framing
Covers practical/logistical topics as general education; gives no financial advice and no medical advice or individual prognosis
Drafted strictly from provided vetted sources (no model-generated facts); marked draft, not published, until dual sign-off
